title: What Florida Changed in Your Sociology Textbook
description: A chapter-by-chapter comparison of the original OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e with Florida's state-modified version, highlighting every edit, deletion, and relocation.

chapters.title: An Introduction to Sociology
chapters.summary_short: The Florida version's most consequential changes fall in Section 1.3, where the entire discussion of critical theory, feminist theory, and critical race theory is deleted from both the main text and t

chapters.title: Sociological Research
chapters.summary_short: The Florida version's most significant deletion is the entire "Critical Sociology" subsection, which explained how scholars including the Frankfurt School and Karl Marx understood research as embedded

chapters.title: Culture / Society and Social Interaction
chapters.summary_short: The Florida version merges original Chapters 3 (Culture) and 4 (Society and Social Interaction) into a single chapter, with substantial condensation: many illustrative sidebars, narrative vignettes, a

chapters.title: Society and Social Interaction
chapters.summary_short: This chapter was merged into Florida Chapter 3 (Culture and Society). See Chapter 3 for a detailed comparison of what content was retained, modified, or removed.

chapters.title: Socialization
chapters.summary_short: The most politically charged change in the Florida version appears at the very start of the chapter: the protagonist Noel, who uses they/them pronouns throughout the OpenStax original, is reassigned f

chapters.title: Groups and Organization
chapters.summary_short: The Florida edition's most significant editorial pattern is removing or softening language that names specific incidents of racism, sexism, or political controversy. Several passages have been deleted

chapters.title: Deviance, Crime, and Social Control
chapters.summary_short: The Florida version's most significant deletion is the passage naming Eric Garner, Breonna Taylor, and George Floyd as victims of police killings. The original stated plainly that none of the officers

chapters.title: Media and Technology
chapters.summary_short: The entire chapter on Media and Technology was removed from the Florida version. The original covered technology's role in society, the digital divide, media consolidation, net neutrality, and theoret

chapters.title: Global Inequality

chapters.title: Race and Ethnicity
chapters.summary_short: The entire chapter on Race and Ethnicity was removed from the Florida version. This is among the most significant removals in the textbook. The original, one of the longest chapters at approximately 1

chapters.title: Gender, Sex, and Sexuality
chapters.summary_short: The entire chapter on Gender, Sex, and Sexuality was removed from the Florida version. The original, approximately 1,550 lines, covered sex and gender as distinct concepts, gender identity and express
